# Draft ECCN Review Memo — Texas Instruments ADC12DJ5200RF Datasheet Sample

## 1. Document Summary
- Title: Texas Instruments ADC12DJ5200RF Datasheet Sample
- Document ID: doc_sample_adc12dj5200rf
- File name: sample-datasheet.txt
- Generated timestamp: 2026-06-17T17:16:54+00:00
- Disclaimer: Draft for expert human review only. This memo is not a final legal or compliance determination.

## 2. Extracted Technical Facts
### Product identity
- **Manufacturer**: Texas Instruments
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "Texas Instruments ADC12DJ5200RF"
- Why it matters: Manufacturer identity anchors the review to the vendor’s own part naming, application framing, and qualification language.

- **Part Number**: ADC12DJ5200RF
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "Texas Instruments ADC12DJ5200RF"
- Why it matters: Part number identification ties the memo to the exact component under review and avoids confusion with reference designs or companion parts.

- **Device Type**: Analog-to-digital converter (ADC)
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "ADC12DJ5200RF 12-bit, 10.4GSPS single-channel or 5.2GSPS dual-channel RF-sampling analog-to-digital converter (ADC)"
- Why it matters: Device type frames which technical parameters are most relevant in the initial review path.
### Converter/performance specs
- **ADC Resolution**: 12 bits
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "ADC12DJ5200RF 12-bit, 10.4GSPS single-channel or 5.2GSPS dual-channel RF-sampling analog-to-digital converter (ADC)"
- Why it matters: ADC resolution matters because converter bit depth, especially when paired with sample rate, is often central to the electronics review path.

- **Channel Modes**: single-channel and dual-channel
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "ADC12DJ5200RF 12-bit, 10.4GSPS single-channel or 5.2GSPS dual-channel RF-sampling analog-to-digital converter (ADC)"
- Why it matters: Channel modes matter because some converter review questions depend on whether the device operates as a single-channel or dual-channel part.

- **Single-Channel Sample Rate**: 10.4 GSPS
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "ADC12DJ5200RF 12-bit, 10.4GSPS single-channel or 5.2GSPS dual-channel RF-sampling analog-to-digital converter (ADC)"
- Why it matters: The single-channel sample rate is one of the key converter performance facts that a reviewer should map to the current Category 3 electronics thresholds.

- **Dual-Channel Sample Rate**: 5.2 GSPS
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "ADC12DJ5200RF 12-bit, 10.4GSPS single-channel or 5.2GSPS dual-channel RF-sampling analog-to-digital converter (ADC)"
- Why it matters: The dual-channel sample rate helps experts assess the alternate operating mode of the reviewed ADC.
### Digital interface/output specs
- **JESD Interface**: JESD204C
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "- JESD204C serial data interface supporting up to 17.16 Gbps line rate"
- Why it matters: The primary JESD output interface matters because serialized converter output format and revision level are often central to the electronics review path.

- **Other JESD References Found**: JESD204B / JESD204A
- Confidence: medium
- Source snippet: "JESD204B and JESD204A references appear in receiver compatibility and subclass discussion, but the device output interface is JESD204C"
- Why it matters: Older JESD references can matter as compatibility notes, but they do not replace the main interface revision used for the review path.

- **Serial Lane Rate**: up to 17.16 Gbps
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "- JESD204C serial data interface supporting up to 17.16 Gbps line rate"
- Why it matters: Serial lane rate matters because high-speed converter output interfaces are often part of the Category 3 electronics review path.

- **Lane Count**: 16 lanes
- Confidence: medium
- Source snippet: "- Serialized digital output supports 16 lanes"
- Why it matters: Lane count helps experts understand the scale of the serialized output architecture.

- **Clocking/Reference Design Note**: For clock frequencies higher than 3.2 GHz, external clock source components may be required in the reference design
- Confidence: low
- Source snippet: "For clock frequencies higher than 3.2 GHz, external clock source components may be required in the reference design"
- Why it matters: This is a clocking or reference-design note, not a processor or device clock-speed claim. It can help reviewers distinguish implementation guidance from the device's own performance specs.
### RF/input-frequency specs
- **Analog Input Bandwidth**: 8 GHz
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "- 12-bit RF-sampling ADC with analog input bandwidth of 8 GHz"
- Why it matters: Analog input bandwidth matters because it shows how far into the RF spectrum the converter is designed to acquire signals.

- **Usable Input Frequency Range**: >10 GHz
- Confidence: high
- Source snippet: "- Usable input frequency range: > 10GHz"
- Why it matters: Usable input frequency range is relevant because it indicates whether the part supports direct RF sampling beyond lower-frequency converter use cases.
### Application/context language
- **Application Examples**: electronic warfare / SIGINT / ELINT / radar
- Confidence: medium
- Source snippet: "Applications include radar, electronic warfare, SIGINT, and ELINT"
- Why it matters: This is application-context language from the datasheet, not a final end-use determination. It may still inform which expert review questions should be asked.
### Power/package specs
- **Package**: FCBGA
- Confidence: medium
- Source snippet: "- Package: FCBGA"
- Why it matters: Package information helps experts identify the physical form factor and deployment context of the reviewed component.

- **Power Consumption**: 4 W
- Confidence: medium
- Source snippet: "- Power consumption: 4 W"
- Why it matters: Power-consumption detail helps reviewers understand the performance density and implementation context of the component.
### Missing/not found facts
- Special-environment qualification details were not found in the provided datasheet text. Radiation, military, space, or extended-temperature language matters because specialized design intent can affect the review path.
- Security or cryptography features were not found in the provided datasheet text. That matters because embedded cryptographic capability can require a different expert review path.

## 3. Candidate ECCN Review Paths
### 3A001 — Category 3 electronics review path for high-speed ADC components
- Confidence: medium
- Why it may apply: The datasheet identifies ADC Resolution: 12 bits, Single-Channel Sample Rate: 10.4 GSPS, Dual-Channel Sample Rate: 5.2 GSPS, Analog Input Bandwidth: 8 GHz, Usable Input Frequency Range: >10 GHz, JESD Interface: JESD204C, Serial Lane Rate: up to 17.16 Gbps. This is a Category 3 electronics review path, not a final ECCN determination. A qualified reviewer must compare these extracted ADC facts against the relevant current CCL thresholds.
- Why it may not apply: The current draft does not encode final legal thresholds or a complete rules engine. A qualified reviewer still needs to map the extracted ADC resolution, sample-rate, bandwidth, frequency-range, and interface facts to the current control text and determine whether a narrower entry is actually triggered.

This draft uses Category 3 as the initial review path because the datasheet contains concrete ADC performance and output-interface facts that should be compared against the current control text by a qualified reviewer.
